_meta.title: Scion Player's Guide: Saints & Monsters
_meta.role: Supplement hooks — Saints (divine-adjacent paths), Titanic Scions, Denizens, and related chargen. Titanic Calling Knacks (Adversary, Destroyer, Monster, Primeval, Tyrant) are merged into knacks from ``data/tables/knacks/20_*.json`` at bundle load.

titanicKnacksCallout: Titanic Calling Knacks from Chapter Four (pp. 91–97) appear in Knacks when your Calling is Adversary, Destroyer, Monster, Primeval, or Tyrant (sm_* ids). Titans Rising adds further Titanic and pantheon-flavored Knacks merged as tr_* (see data/tables/knacks/30_*.json).
saintsCallout: Saint chargen, Guides, Relics, and Marvel/Boon limits are PDF-first; extend data/tables/boons/ fragments or dedicated tables when you transcribe rules from the Saints chapters.
sorcererDataNote: Magic Purview and sample Magic Boons merge from ``data/tables/purviews/15_*.json`` and ``data/tables/boons/20_*.json`` at bundle load. Sorcerer wizard fields live in character.sorceryProfile (export JSON).

inherent.name: Spite
inherent.hintDescription: Malice at a glance — imposes misfortune or injury on one target through words or a glare (Saints & Monsters Binding).
inherent.hintMechanics: Skill roll yes; scene; one subject; Short range; pay 1 Momentum or 1 Legend depending on branch (see book). Injury track: spend 1 Momentum and roll Sorcery as the attack to start; further Spite on that same target this scene is free. Stunts: forced eye contact (limits actions), Injuries (Aggravated at higher successes), knock prone, or spend Legend for a Howl vs everyone in the target’s range band. Misfortune track: spend 1 Legend to impose half your successes as Difficulty on all of the target’s rolls for the scene (max 5), or 1 Momentum to impose full successes on one roll they choose this scene (max 5).

additional.name: Curse
additional.hintDescription: Places a named harmful Condition on a target for the Working’s curse-and-hex toolkit.
additional.hintMechanics: Sorcery roll; imbue 1 Legend; scene; Short range. You must define the curse before rolling; the SG sets difficulty from how heavy the effect is. Purely narrative Conditions use the guidance in Origin (around p. 64); when a Condition is expressed as added Difficulty, the penalty equals half your rolled successes. The book also describes a ritual to hang a curse on an object with a simple trigger until removed (costs 1 Legend there).

additional.name: Draw Out
additional.hintDescription: The “love spell” mortals talk about — it does not create affection; it compels the victim to come to you while they waste away until they arrive.
additional.hintMechanics: Sorcery roll; imbue 1 Legend; indefinite; one character; Long range. Until the target reaches you they gain Wasting (no food or sleep) and rising Difficulty on all actions (+1 after a day, +2 after three days, +4 after a week, then daily Injuries until resolved). Cannot target anyone of a higher tier. Equal-tier targets may roll Resilience + Resolve each day against difficulty equal to your original spell successes to break free.

additional.name: Envessel
additional.hintDescription: Binds a spirit into an object — common paired play with Summoning for bottled ghosts and bound helpers.
additional.hintMechanics: No roll, no cost, scene, one spirit at Short range if the spirit is already Taken Out. Active spirits resist with Sorcery vs. difficulty equal to their remaining Health, spending 1 Momentum per attempt. While trapped you may interrogate them once per session, or release them to perform one service (they often resent this; you may optionally take a Nemesis Fatebinding right after the service).

additional.name: Spirit-Wrestling
additional.hintDescription: Drives a spirit from your presence for the session through a stylized contest (riddles, dance, literal wrestling, etc.).
additional.hintMechanics: Imbue 1 Legend; arc duration; one spirit; Short range. Roll complexity varies: contest is a complex action using a skill the spirit picks, with about 1–4 Milestones keyed to spirit Archetype rank (±2 Milestones if the spirit is one tier above/below you, minimum 1). You must finish the contest: win and it leaves; lose and you cannot use this Technique on that spirit again this arc; interruption lets you recast but resets progress or counts as a loss depending on who was ahead.

additional.name: Forbiddance
additional.hintDescription: Like a geas — forbids one consciously controllable action (e.g. “do not move”), not impossible physiology (“do not breathe”).
additional.hintMechanics: Sorcery roll; 1 Momentum; scene; one character; Short range. On success applies the Forbidden Condition for the scene or until you end it, blocking the named action.

additional.name: Subversion
additional.hintDescription: Bends how a Storyguide character feels about you — not mind control, but a shift in Attitude in your favor or fear.
additional.hintMechanics: Sorcery roll; 1 Legend; scene; one character; Short range. Name the Attitude level you want (0–5, positive or negative); difficulty equals how far that is from their current Attitude. Cannot target anyone two or more tiers above you.

sorcererWorkingsCatalog.name: Binding
sorcererWorkingsCatalog.summary: Curses, love-magic, geasa, and hostile conditions — charms hinder briefly; Techniques include oaths and spirit-binds (S&M pp. 66–68).

sorcererWorkingsCatalog.name: Divination
sorcererWorkingsCatalog.summary: Omens, research shortcuts, and truth-seeking — inherent Read the Signs; other Techniques extend what you can learn (S&M from p. 68 onward).

sorcererWorkingsCatalog.name: Summoning
sorcererWorkingsCatalog.summary: Calling, bargaining with, and banishing spirits and other entities — overlaps with other Workings when used as rituals (S&M from p. 63 onward).

sorcererWorkingsCatalog.name: Wonderment
sorcererWorkingsCatalog.summary: Illusions, conjured props, and unreal vistas — Techniques build toward greater unreal creations (S&M from p. 64 onward).

sorcererWorkingsCatalog.name: Shapechanging
sorcererWorkingsCatalog.summary: Altered forms and bodies — inherent and Techniques cover partial shifts, animal shapes, and stranger transformations (S&M from p. 66 onward).

titanicDataNote: Epicenter summaries: bundle.epicenters (after load). Titanic extras: character.titanicProfile.
